conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like watching someone else live my life. I look at people moving around, and I feel like I’m just stuck on the sidelines.
You mentioned feeling stuck on the sidelines—has that made it hard to care about things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, it has. I used to love reading, but now it feels pointless. I don't know why I'm wasting my time with it when I can't even enjoy it.
When you say reading feels pointless, what does that make you think about yourself?
It makes me think I’m done. Like, I've lived my life and now I’m just going through the motions.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It’s been building for a while. After the divorce, things changed, and I couldn’t get the old me back.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
The evenings. I sit there, and I have nothing to look forward to. No one to call, no plans... just silence.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I feel like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but I have no idea what that is.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
I don't know if it's giving up or just accepting that this is my life now. Either way, it feels like the end of something, even if I don't know what that something is.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I don't think anyone would notice if I wasn't here. It's not about anyone else, it's just... I don't have anything left to give, and I don't know how to fix that.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to find the motivation for stuff you usually enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't be bothered. It feels like too much effort to get out there and enjoy it when I’m just going to be thinking about the past.
When you're thinking about the past at night, what kinds of things do you find yourself focusing on?
Just the little things, like how I used to be so much more confident before. Now I feel like I’m just trying to hold it together.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like walking around with a weight on my chest. I just feel so... heavy, you know? Like I’m carrying this constant sadness around with me.
How do you see things going for you in the near future?
Honestly, I don't know. I’m scared that I’m going to lose my job because I’m not performing well, and then I'll be stuck in a small flat, just thinking about it all the time.
You mentioned feeling heavy and carrying a constant sadness—does that ever make you feel more on edge or irritable with people around you?
Yeah, it does. I try to hide it, but I know I’m snapping at my friends, and I hate that.
When you snap at your friends, what happens after that?
They try to talk to me about it, but I just shut down. I don't want to be that person, but I don't know how to stop it from happening.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I guess just that I feel like I'm losing myself. I used to be the one who was always positive and happy, and now I'm just... broken.
When you say you feel broken, what does that feel like on a typical day?
It feels like I’m just going through the motions. I show up at work, but I’m just waiting for the day to be over so I can go back to my flat and cry.
